title: Hut 8 Q1 2026 slides: $16.8B in AI data center leases amid losses
text: Hut 8 reported $16.8 billion in contracted AI data center leases in its Q1 2026 presentation despite reporting significant losses in the period. The apparent contradiction highlights the tension between revenue growth and profitability in the company's business model. Contracted Revenue Base: Hut 8's $16.8 billion in committed lease contracts provides substantial multi-year revenue visibility. This contracted base spans 597 MW of IT capacity across multiple campuses. Revenue Recognition: While the contracts are substantial, revenue recognition timing and margin realization are key questions for investors. The company will gradually recognize revenue as facilities are constructed and come online. Loss Explanation: The substantial Q1 loss of $253.1 million was primarily driven by unrealized mark-to-market adjustments on digital assets (Bitcoin holdings), not operational losses. The company's adjusted EBITDA loss reflected high development and construction costs. Forward Trajectory: As contracted projects come online and revenue recognition accelerates, the company's profitability profile is expected to improve significantly. Management has provided guidance for near-term losses but expects a return to profitability as major projects deliver revenue.
